description: This jet propulsion module is a concentrated embodiment of military-grade aerospace engineering.  Originally designed for use as the primary propulsion system and altitude control mechanism in next-generation jet fighters, it boasts an overwhelming thrust-to-weight ratio that far surpasses that of conventional internal combustion engines.
This is a vertical takeoff and landing (VTOL) propulsion system used in military VTOL aircraft.  It provides both lift for taking off and thrust when in the air.

This is a lightweight hover repulsor module mounted on the underside of a vehicle.  It offers unparalleled maneuverability, allowing the vehicle to glide and fly effortlessly over rough terrain and obstacles.  Though originally intended to be frictionless, unknown antigrav phenomena nonetheless causes vehicles to experience a force similar to friction.
